label: EasyPower - Arc Flash Hazard Report spreadsheet (Open With Excel)
toolVersionRange: EasyPower ~9.x-12.x Arc Flash Hazard Report. Column captions verified against EasyPower's official help topic 'Arc Flash Hazard Reports' (help.easypower.com, EasyPower 10.3, help updated 2020-12-04), including its 'Spreadsheet Column Headings' table and report screenshots. Newer releases (Bentley) may add columns - confirm against one real export.
policyNote: LIABILITY POSTURE: ServiceCycle stores PE-stamped study RESULTS; it never computes incident energy and never asserts PPE categories. EasyPower's computed PPE columns ('PPE Level', 'PPE Description', legacy dual-purpose 'Comments/PPE Level') are deliberately mapped to NOTHING by this template. Unit conversions here are mechanical only (kV to V, seconds to ms, J/cm2 to cal/cm2); no engineering derivation is performed.
sourceNotes: PRIMARY (verified): EasyPower 10.3 help topic 'Arc Flash Hazard Reports' (help.easypower.com/ezp/10.3/Content/16_Arc_Flash_Hazard_Analysis/Arc_Flash_Hazard_Report.htm) - report screenshots show the on-screen captions (Arc Fault Bus Name / Arc Fault Bus kV / Upstream Trip Device Name / Upstream Trip Device Function / Equip Type / Electrode Configuration / Electrode Gap (mm) / Bus Bolted Fault (kA) / Bus Arc Fault (kA) / Trip Time (sec) / Opening Time (sec) / Arc Time (sec) / Est Arc Flash Boundary (inches) / Working Distance (inches) / Incident Energy (cal/cm2) / Comments) and the 'Spreadsheet Column Headings' table enumerates every optional column.
Same help topic: 'Opening the Arc Flash Hazard Report in Excel' documents that this exact spreadsheet is what exports to Excel.

Unit caveats from the help text: Electrode Gap 'is always expressed in millimeters'; Incident Energy 'is reported in calories or joules per square centimeter based on settings'; boundary/working-distance units follow Short Circuit Options. Bare (unit-less) captions are therefore left unmapped as ambiguous rather than guessed.

mappings.source: EasyPower 10.3 help - default column; described as 'the ID name of the buses faulted'.
EasyPower 10.3 help - default column; 'the voltage of the bus being faulted' (values like 0.48, 13.8).
EasyPower 10.3 help - default column; the protective device whose trip time drives the row.
mappings.note: Device NAME/designation, not a catalog model number; mapped to deviceModel for parity with the existing AFX crosswalk.

mappings.source: EasyPower 10.3 help - 'Equipment Type' in the headings table; report screenshots abbreviate the caption to 'Equip Type'. Values: Switchgear, MCC/Panel, Open Air, Conductor.
EasyPower 10.3 help - default column, IEEE 1584-2018 only (values VCB, VOA, ...).
EasyPower 10.3 help - default column; help states the gap 'is always expressed in millimeters', so the unit-less caption is safe to map.
EasyPower 10.3 help - default column; 'total bolted fault current calculated at the faulted bus'.
mappings.note: Bus-level value. 'Branch Bolted Fault (kA)' (through-device current) is intentionally NOT mapped.

mappings.source: EasyPower 10.3 help - default column; 'total arcing current calculated at the faulted bus'.
EasyPower 10.3 help - default column; defined as 'total duration of arc fault... the sum of Trip Time and Opening Time' - i.e. the total clearing time.
mappings.note: Arc Time is already the tool-defined TOTAL (trip + opening), so no summing is needed. The separate Trip Time / Opening Time columns are recognized but unmapped.

mappings.source: EasyPower 10.3 help report screenshots show 'Est Arc Flash Boundary (inches)'; the headings table calls it 'Estimated Arc Flash Boundary' with units set in Short Circuit Options.
mappings.note: Only the explicit inches captions are mapped. The bare 'Estimated Arc Flash Boundary' caption is left unmapped because its unit follows a project setting.

mappings.source: EasyPower 10.3 help report screenshots show 'Working Distance (inches)'.
mappings.note: Bare 'Working Distance' caption is left unmapped (unit follows a project setting). EasyPower can emit one row per working distance (up to five); duplicate-bus rows are surfaced by the downstream multi-table validation for engineer review.

mappings.source: EasyPower 10.3 help report screenshots show 'Incident Energy (cal/cm2)'.

EasyPower 10.3 help states incident energy 'is reported in calories or joules per square centimeter based on settings in Short Circuit Options'; the exact J/cm2 caption has not been sighted in an export.
mappings.note: Mechanical conversion: J/cm2 / 4.184 = cal/cm2 (thermochemical calorie, the IEEE 1584 convention).

ignoredByPolicy.note: EasyPower's PPE columns display the software-selected PPE level/clothing description from its device library ('PPE Levels' section). Recorded product policy: ServiceCycle never asserts PPE categories; tool-computed PPE columns are mapped to nothing. The legacy dual-purpose 'Comments/PPE Level' column is dropped for the same reason (it may carry PPE levels).

knownUnmapped.note: Component times. 'Arc Time (sec)' is the tool-defined total and is what maps to clearingTimeMs; ServiceCycle does not sum or reconcile the component columns.
Through-device (branch) currents; AFX v1 carries the bus-level values only.

DELIBERATELY unmapped: these bare captions take their unit from Short Circuit Options (cal vs J, inches vs other), so mapping them would guess units. Re-export with explicit units or use the AFX headers.
NFPA 70E shock-protection boundaries; AFX v1 has no shock-boundary fields.

Optional EasyPower report columns with no AFX v1 equivalent (locations, relay function IDs, grounding flag, enclosure dimensions - AFX uses an enclosure class, not dimensions). Listed so they are recognized rather than 'unknown'.
Free-text comments and label print-quantity plumbing; no AFX v1 equivalent.
